description: Get basic summary of anomaly detection results

example.code:
# After running any anomaly detection function
summary = anomaly_pipe.get_anomaly_summary()
print(f"Total anomalies: {summary['total_anomalies']}")
print(f"Anomaly percentage: {summary['total_anomaly_percentage']:.2f}%")
print(f"Detection method: {summary['detection_method']}")
print(f"Analysis name: {summary['analysis_name']}")

# After running any anomaly detection function
top_anomalies = anomaly_pipe.get_top_anomalies(n=10, time_column='timestamp')
print("Top 10 Anomalies:")
print(top_anomalies[['timestamp', 'value', 'anomaly_score']].head())

# Get top anomalies for specific analysis
top_contextual = anomaly_pipe.get_top_anomalies(
    n=5, 
    analysis_name='contextual_anomalies_residual_ewm'
)
print("Top 5 Contextual Anomalies:")
print(top_contextual.head())

parameters.n: Number of top anomalies to return (default: 10)
parameters.analysis_name: Specific analysis to get anomalies from (optional)

description: Export anomaly detection results to DataFrame

example.code:
# After running any anomaly detection function
result_df = anomaly_pipe.to_df()
print(f"Result shape: {result_df.shape}")
print(f"Anomaly columns: {[col for col in result_df.columns if 'anomaly' in col]}")
print(result_df.head())

parameters.analysis_name: Name of the specific analysis to convert (optional)
parameters.include_metadata: Whether to include metadata columns (default: False)
parameters.include_original: Whether to include original data columns (default: True)

parameters.analysis_name: Name of the specific analysis to convert (optional)
parameters.include_metadata: Whether to include metadata columns (default: False)
parameters.include_original: Whether to include original data columns (default: True)

parameters.analysis_name: Name of the specific analysis to convert (optional)
parameters.include_metadata: Whether to include metadata columns (default: False)
parameters.include_original: Whether to include original data columns (default: True)

description: Merge anomaly results back to original data

example.code:
# After running any anomaly detection function
original_data = sample_data.copy()
enriched_data = anomaly_pipe.merge_to_df(original_data)
print(f"Enriched data shape: {enriched_data.shape}")
print(f"New columns: {[col for col in enriched_data.columns if col not in original_data.columns]}")
print(enriched_data.head())

parameters.base_df: The base dataframe to merge results into
parameters.analysis_name: Name of the specific analysis to merge (optional)
parameters.include_metadata: Whether to include metadata columns (default: False)
